summary: Read-only audit of Codex UserPromptSubmit pre-response DinoBrain preflight enforcement. Found that the installed hook is configured and the wrapper/probe path passes, including fail-closed behavior inside the hook, but live verification found no Codex Desktop UserPromptSubmit preflight event for the current prompt or any prompt after 2026-07-07T02:00:00Z. Docs and scripts explicitly acknowledge trust and stale-session reload gaps, so every new Codex user prompt is not mechanically guaranteed to run DinoBrain preflight before response in current-session reload/trust edge cases.
decisions: Verdict FAIL for the strong claim that every new Codex user prompt must run DinoBrain preflight before response.
Treat configured/probe-verified hooks as insufficient live proof; require verify:codex-live evidence from a fresh trusted Codex Desktop session.
Keep read-only audit scope; no code edits made.
next_steps: Trust the DinoBrain UserPromptSubmit hook in /hooks if pending, fully restart Codex or open a new trusted thread, then rerun verify:codex-live with a unique prompt snippet.
Consider adding a fail-closed agent-side/session guard for missing live preflight context if the product requirement is absolute enforcement across trust/reload edge cases.
search_queries: Codex pre-response UserPromptSubmit hook enforcement, preflight before response, current-session reload/trust edge cases, hook config and verification surfaces
